steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Since both terms share $(2)^{x}$: $$f(x)=20(2)^{x}+6(2)^{x}=26(2)^{x}.$$ The coefficient $26$ now equals the y-intercept.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
We need a form that displays $26$ as a coefficient or base.
  • A) $80(2)^{x-2}+24(2)^{x-2}$ — equivalent, but $26$ does not appear; $f(0)=80/4+24/4=20+6=26$ is hidden.
  • B) $2(10(2)^{x}+3(2)^{x})$ — coefficients $2,10,3$; $26$ is not displayed.
  • C) $20(4)^{x/2}+6(4)^{x/2}$ — uses base $4$; $26$ is not displayed.
  • D) $26(2)^{x}$ — the coefficient $26$ is exactly the y-intercept.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
